sections.title: PURPOSE AND SCOPE
sections.llm_prompt: Write Purpose and Scope for a stability study on {product_name}. State objectives (establish shelf-life/retest period, support storage conditions, support labeling, detect degradation products), the dosage form, packaging, and number of batches. Distinguish primary (registration) from supportive stability. Reference ICH Q1A(R2) or Q5C. {description}

sections.title: STUDY DESIGN
sections.llm_prompt: Describe the study design for {product_name} stability. Include: number of batches (typically 3 for registration), batch representativeness (pilot or commercial scale), container closure, orientation (upright/inverted), bracketing/matrixing if applicable (ICH Q1D), placebo if applicable.

sections.title: STORAGE CONDITIONS
sections.llm_prompt: Generate the storage conditions table per ICH Q1A(R2). Columns: Condition | Temperature | Relative Humidity | Purpose | Duration. Include long-term, intermediate (if needed), accelerated, photostability (Q1B), and any cell-therapy-specific ultra-low conditions (-80°C, LN2 vapor phase, -150°C).

sections.title: TESTING INTERVALS
sections.llm_prompt: Generate the testing intervals table. Columns: Condition | T=0 | 1mo | 3mo | 6mo | 9mo | 12mo | 18mo | 24mo | 36mo. Mark cells with X for testing time points per ICH Q1A(R2) and reduced schedules for accelerated conditions.

sections.title: TEST PARAMETERS AND ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
sections.llm_prompt: Generate the stability test parameters table for {product_name}. Columns: Test | Method (SOP/USP) | Acceptance Criteria | Stability Indicating (Y/N). Include: Appearance, Assay/Potency, Related Substances/Impurities, pH, Dissolution (solid dosage), Water Content, Particulate Matter, Microbial/Sterility, Container Closure Integrity, and product-specific attributes.

sections.title: SAMPLE MANAGEMENT
sections.llm_prompt: Describe sample management for the stability program. Include: sample quantity calculation, labeling, inventory tracking, chamber assignment, chamber monitoring (temperature/humidity continuous monitoring with alarms), chamber excursion handling, sample retrieval and testing workflow.

sections.title: DATA ANALYSIS AND SHELF-LIFE DETERMINATION
sections.llm_prompt: Describe data analysis and shelf-life determination. Reference ICH Q1E: statistical analysis of quantitative data (ANCOVA with poolability testing), extrapolation rules, graphical evaluation, shelf-life proposal as lower 95% confidence bound where assay intersects specification. Describe how significant change is determined (e.g., 5% assay loss, specification failure, pH shift).

sections.title: HANDLING OF OUT-OF-SPECIFICATION RESULTS
sections.llm_prompt: Describe how OOS and OOT results in stability will be handled. Reference the OOS SOP, root cause investigation approach, potential retesting, impact on shelf-life, notification to QA and regulatory, and CAPA.

sections.title: REFERENCES
sections.llm_prompt: Generate references for a stability protocol. Include: ICH Q1A(R2), Q1B, Q1C, Q1D, Q1E, Q5C (biologics), Q3B(R2) impurities, USP <1150>, 21 CFR 211.166, 21 CFR 211.170, FDA stability guidance, and relevant company SOPs.

metadata.regulatory_framework: ICH Q1A(R2), ICH Q1B-E, ICH Q5C, 21 CFR 211.166, 21 CFR 211.170
